content.title: Passage 1

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Hearing impairment or other auditory function deficit in young children can have a major impact on their development of speech and communication, resulting in a detrimental effect on their ability to learn at school. This is likely to have major consequences for the individual and the population as a whole. The New Zealand Ministry of Health has found from research carried out over two decades that 6-10% of children in that country are affected by hearing loss.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: A preliminary study in New Zealand has shown that classroom noise presents a major concern for teachers and pupils. Modern teaching practices, the organization of desks in the classroom, poor classroom acoustics, and mechanical means of ventilation such as air-conditioning units all contribute to the number of children unable to comprehend the teacher's voice. Education researchers Nelson and Soli have also suggested that recent trends in learning often involve collaborative interaction of multiple minds and tools as much as individual possession of information. This all amounts to heightened activity and noise levels, which have the potential to be particularly serious for children experiencing auditory function deficit. Noise in classrooms can only exacerbate their difficulty in comprehending and processing verbal communication with other children and instructions from the teacher.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Children with auditory function deficit are potentially failing to learn to their maximum potential because of noise levels generated in classrooms. The effects of noise on the ability of children to learn effectively in typical classroom environments are now the subjects of increasing concern. The International Institute of Noise Control Engineering (I-INCE), on the advice of the World Health Organization, has established an international working party, which includes New Zealand, to evaluate noise and reverberation control for school rooms.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: While the detrimental effects of noise in classroom situations are not limited to children experiencing disability, those with a disability that affects their processing of speech and verbal communication could be extremely vulnerable. The auditory function deficits in question include hearing impairment, autistic spectrum disorders (ASD) and attention deficit disorders (ADD/ADHD).

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Autism is considered a neurological and genetic life-long disorder that causes discrepancies in the way information is processed. This disorder is characterised by interlinking problems with social imagination, social communication and social interaction. According to Janzen, this affects the ability to understand and relate in typical ways to people, understand events and objects in the environment, and understand or respond to sensory stimuli. Autism does not allow learning or thinking in the same ways as in children who are developing normally. Autistic spectrum disorders often result in major difficulties in comprehending verbal information and speech processing. Those experiencing these disorders often find sounds such as crowd noise and the noise generated by machinery painful and distressing. This is difficult to scientifically quantify as such extra-sensory stimuli vary greatly from one autistic individual to another. But a child who finds any type of noise in their classroom or learning space intrusive is likely to be adversely affected in their ability to process information.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The attention deficit disorders are indicative of neurological and genetic disorders and are characterised by difficulties with sustaining attention, effort and persistence, organization skills and disinhibition. Children experiencing these disorders find it difficult to screen out unimportant information, and focus on everything in the environment rather than attending to a single activity. Background noise in the classroom becomes a major distraction, which can affect their ability to concentrate.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Children experiencing an auditory function deficit can often find speech and communication very difficult to isolate and process when set against high levels of background noise. These levels come from outside activities that penetrate the classroom structure, from teaching activities, and other noise generated inside, which can be exacerbated by room reverberation. Strategies are needed to obtain the optimum classroom construction and perhaps a change in classroom culture and methods of teaching. In particular, the effects of noisy classrooms and activities on those experiencing disabilities in the form of auditory function deficit need thorough investigation. It is probable that many undiagnosed children exist in the education system with 'invisible' disabilities. Their needs are less likely to be met than those of children with known disabilities.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The New Zealand Government has developed a New Zealand Disability Strategy and has embarked on a wide-ranging consultation process. The strategy recognises that people experiencing disability face significant barriers in achieving a full quality of life in areas such as attitude, education, employment and access to services. Objective 3 of the New Zealand Disability Strategy is to 'Provide the Best Education for Disabled People' by improving education so that all children, youth learners and adult learners will have equal opportunities to learn and develop within their already existing local school. For a successful education, the learning environment is vitally significant, so any effort to improve this is likely to be of great benefit to all children, but especially to those with auditory function disabilities.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: A number of countries are already in the process of formulating their own standards for the control and reduction of classroom noise. New Zealand will probably follow their example. The literature to date on noise in school rooms appears to focus on the effects on schoolchildren in general, their teachers and the hearing impaired. Only limited attention appears to have been given to those students experiencing the other disabilities involving auditory function deficit. It is imperative that the needs of these children are taken into account in the setting of appropriate international standards to be promulgated in future.

groups.desc:
Questions 1-6

Reading Passage 1 has nine sections, A-I.
Which section contains the following information?
Choose the correct answer.

questions.title: an account of a national policy initiative
questions.analysis: 段落H详细描述了新西兰政府制定的残疾战略及其具体目标和实施过程，这正是题干中“an account of a national policy initiative”的具体体现。因此，段落H是最直接对应题干信息的段落。

central_sentences.sentence: The New Zealand Government has developed a New Zealand Disability Strategy and has embarked on a wide-ranging consultation process.
central_sentences.translation: 新西兰政府制定了一项新西兰残疾战略，并开始了一个广泛的咨询过程。

target_sen.target_sen: The New Zealand Government has developed a New Zealand Disability Strategy and has embarked on a wide-ranging consultation process.

Objective 3 of the New Zealand Disability Strategy is to 'Provide the Best Education for Disabled People'by improving education so that all children, youth learners and adult learners will have equal opportunities to learn and develop within their already existing local school.
The New Zealand Government has developed a New Zealand Disability Strategy and has embarked on a wide-ranging consultation process.

Objective 3 of the New Zealand Disability Strategy is to 'Provide the Best Education for Disabled People'by improving education so that all children, youth learners and adult learners will have equal opportunities to learn and develop within their already existing local school.
The New Zealand Government has developed a New Zealand Disability Strategy and has embarked on a wide-ranging consultation process.

questions.title: a description of a global team effort
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落C，因为段落C中提到的“an international working party”与题干中的“a global team effort”意思相同，都是指全球范围内的团队合作。段落C详细描述了这个国际工作组的成立及其目的，完全符合题干要求。

central_sentences.sentence: The International Institute of Noise Control Engineering (I-INCE), on the advice of the World Health Organization, has established an international working party, which includes New Zealand, to evaluate noise and reverberation control for school rooms.
central_sentences.translation: 在世界卫生组织的建议下，国际噪音控制工程学会（I-INCE）建立了一个包括新西兰在内的国际工作组，以评估学校教室的噪音和回声控制。

target_sen.target_sen: The International Institute of Noise Control Engineering (I-INCE), on the advice of the World Health Organization, has established an international working party, which includes New Zealand, to evaluate noise and reverberation control for school rooms.

The International Institute of Noise Control Engineering (I-INCE), on the advice of the World Health Organization, has established an international working party, which includes New Zealand, to evaluate noise and reverberation control for school rooms.

questions.title: a hypothesis as to one reason behind the growth in classroom noise
questions.analysis: 段落B中明确提到“recent trends in learning often involve collaborative interaction of multiple minds and tools”，这解释了课堂噪音增加的原因。题干中的“hypothesis”对应“suggested”，“reason”对应“contribute to”，“growth in classroom noise”对应“heightened activity and noise levels”。因此，段落B是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Education researchers Nelson and Soli have also suggested that recent trends in learning often involve collaborative interaction of multiple minds and tools as much as individual possession of information. This all amounts to heightened activity and noise levels, which have the potential to be particularly serious for children experiencing auditory function deficit.
central_sentences.translation: 教育研究人员Nelson和Soli还提出，近期的学习趋势常常涉及多个头脑和工具的协作互动，而不仅仅是个人对信息的掌握。这一切都导致了活动和噪音水平的提高，这对于有听觉功能缺陷的儿童来说可能特别严重。

target_sen.target_sen: Education researchers Nelson and Soli have also suggested that recent trends in learning often involve collaborative interaction of multiple minds and tools as much as individual possession of information.

Modern teaching practices, the organizations of desks in the classroom, poor classroom acoustics, and mechanical means of ventilation such as air-conditioning units all contribute to the number of children unable to comprehend the teacher's voice.
This all amounts to heightened activity and noise levels, which have the potential to be particularly serious for children experiencing auditory function deficit.

questions.title: a demand for suitable worldwide regulations
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落I。题目中的“a demand for suitable worldwide regulations”在段落I中有明确的同义替换表达：“imperative”和“international standards”。段落I提到“必须在未来制定适当的国际标准时考虑这些孩子的需求”，这与题目中的“对适当的全球性法规的需求”完全对应。

central_sentences.sentence: It is imperative that the needs of these children are taken into account in the setting of appropriate international standards to be promulgated in future.
central_sentences.translation: 在未来制定适当的国际标准时，必须考虑这些儿童的需求，这一点至关重要。

target_sen.target_sen: It is imperative that the needs of these children are taken into account in the setting of appropriate international standards to be promulgated in future.
A number of countries are already in the process of formulating their own standards for the control and reduction of classroom noise.
It is imperative that the needs of these children are taken into account in the setting of appropriate international standards to be promulgated in future.

questions.title: a list of medical conditions which place some children more at risk from noise than others
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落D，因为段落D明确列出了多种医学状况（听力损伤、自闭症谱系障碍和注意力缺陷障碍），并且指出这些状况使得一些孩子比其他孩子更容易受到噪音的影响。题目中的“medical conditions”在段落D中对应的是“auditory function deficits”，而“more at risk from noise”对应的是“extremely vulnerable”。

central_sentences.sentence: The auditory function deficits in question include hearing impairment, autistic spectrum disorders (ASD) and attention deficit disorders (ADD/ADHD).
central_sentences.translation: 所讨论的听觉功能缺陷包括听力损伤、自闭症谱系障碍（ASD）和注意力缺陷障碍（ADD/ADHD）。

target_sen.target_sen: The auditory function deficits in question include hearing impairment, autistic spectrum disorders (ASD) and attention deficit disorders (ADD/ADHD).

While the detrimental effects of noise in classroom situations are not limited to children experiencing disability, those with a disability that affects their processing of speech and verbal communication could be extremely vulnerable.

questions.title: the estimated proportion of children in New Zealand with auditory problems
questions.analysis:
题干中的“the estimated proportion of children in New Zealand with auditory problems”可以通过以下同义替换词在原文中找到对应关系：
- “estimated proportion”对应“6-10%”
- “children in New Zealand”对应“children in that country”
- “auditory problems”对应“hearing loss”

段落A中明确提到“6-10% of children in that country are affected by hearing loss”，这与题干信息完全匹配，因此答案是段落A。

central_sentences.sentence: The New Zealand Ministry of Health has found from research carried out over two decades that 6-10% of children in that country are affected by hearing loss.
central_sentences.translation: 新西兰卫生部通过二十多年的研究发现，该国6-10%的儿童受到听力损失的影响。

target_sen.target_sen: The New Zealand Ministry of Health has found from research carried out over two decades that 6-10% of children in that country are affected by hearing loss.

Hearing impairment or other auditory function deficit in young children can have a major impact on their development of speech and communication, resulting in a detrimental effect on their ability to learn at school.
The New Zealand Ministry of Health has found from research carried out over two decades that 6-10% of children in that country are affected by hearing loss.
The New Zealand Ministry of Health has found from research carried out over two decades that 6-10% of children in that country are affected by hearing loss.
The New Zealand Ministry of Health has found from research carried out over two decades that 6-10% of children in that country are affected by hearing loss.

groups.desc:
Questions 7-10

Answer the questions below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER in each gap.

questions.title: For what period of time has hearing loss in schoolchildren been studied in New Zealand?
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 "two decades"。题干中的线索词 "period of time" 在原文中对应的同义替换词是 "over two decades"。通过识别题干中的关键词并在文章中找到对应的同义表达，可以准确定位到答案所在的句子。即使词汇量有限，学生也可以通过关键词定位和时间表达来找到正确答案。避免常见错误的关键在于理解题干问的是时间长度，而不是其他信息。

central_sentences.sentence: The New Zealand Ministry of Health has found from research carried out over two decades that 6-10% of children in that country are affected by hearing loss.
central_sentences.translation: 新西兰卫生部通过长达二十年的研究发现，该国6-10%的儿童受到听力损失的影响。

target_sen.target_sen: Hearing impairment or other auditory function deficit in young children can have a major impact on their development of speech and communication, resulting in a detrimental effect on their ability to learn at school.
The New Zealand Ministry of Health has found from research carried out over two decades that 6-10% of children in that country are affected by hearing loss.
The New Zealand Ministry of Health has found from research carried out over two decades that 6-10% of children in that country are affected by hearing loss.
The New Zealand Ministry of Health has found from research carried out over two decades that 6-10% of children in that country are affected by hearing loss.
The New Zealand Ministry of Health has found from research carried out over two decades that 6-10% of children in that country are affected by hearing loss.

questions.title: In addition to machinery noise, what other type of noise can upset children with autism?
questions.analysis: 题干问的是除了机械噪音之外，还有什么类型的噪音会让自闭症儿童感到不安。通过题干中的线索词 "machinery noise" 和 "upset children with autism"，我们在原文中找到了对应的句子。原文中提到 "crowd noise" 和 "the noise generated by machinery" 都会让这些孩子感到 "painful and distressing"。因此，答案是 "crowd/crowd noise"。

central_sentences.sentence: Those experiencing these disorders often find sounds such as crowd noise and the noise generated by machinery painful and distressing.
central_sentences.translation: 患有这些障碍的人通常会发现人群噪音和机器产生的噪音令人痛苦和苦恼。

target_sen.target_sen: Those experiencing these disorders often find sounds such as crowd noise and the noise generated by machinery painful and distressing.
Those experiencing these disorders often find sounds such as crowd noise and the noise generated by machinery painful and distressing.

questions.title: What term is used to describe the hearing problems of schoolchildren which have not been diagnosed?
questions.analysis: 答案是 "invisible/invisible disabilities/invisible disability"，因为文章中明确提到 "undiagnosed children" 存在 "invisible disabilities"。通过识别题干中的同义表达 "hearing problems" 对应 "disabilities" 和 "not been diagnosed" 对应 "undiagnosed"，可以定位到答案句。答案句中的 "invisible disabilities" 是用来描述这些未被诊断的听力问题的术语，因此是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: It is probable that many undiagnosed children exist in the education system with 'invisible' disabilities.
central_sentences.translation: 教育系统中可能存在许多未经诊断的儿童，他们有"隐形"的残疾。

target_sen.target_sen: In particular, the effects of noisy classrooms and activities on those experiencing disabilities in the form of auditory function deficit need thorough investigation.
It is probable that many undiagnosed children exist in the education system with 'invisible' disabilities.
Their needs are less likely to be met than those of children with known disabilities.
It is probable that many undiagnosed children exist in the education system with 'invisible' disabilities.

questions.title: What part of the New Zealand Disability Strategy aims to give schoolchildren equal opportunity?
questions.analysis: 答案是“Objective 3”，因为文章明确指出，新西兰残疾人战略的目标3旨在通过改善教育，使所有学生有平等的学习机会。这与题干中的“equal opportunity”直接对应。通过找到题干中的关键词“New Zealand Disability Strategy”和“equal opportunity”，可以在文章中定位到相关句子，从而确定答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Objective 3 of the New Zealand Disability Strategy is to 'Provide the Best Education for Disabled People'by improving education so that all children, youth learners and adult learners will have equal opportunities to learn and develop within their already existing local school. For
central_sentences.translation: 新西兰残疾人战略的目标3是"为残疾人提供最佳教育"，通过改善教育，使所有儿童、青年学习者和成人学习者能够在他们现有的当地学校中获得平等的学习和发展机会。

target_sen.target_sen: The New Zealand Government has developed a New Zealand Disability Strategy and has embarked on a wide-ranging consultation process.

Objective 3 of the New Zealand Disability Strategy is to 'Provide the Best Education for Disabled People'by improving education so that all children, youth learners and adult learners will have equal opportunities to learn and develop within their already existing local school.

syn_ary.synonym: equal opportunities to learn and develop

target_sen.target_sen: Objective 3 of the New Zealand Disability Strategy is to 'Provide the Best Education for Disabled People'by improving education so that all children, youth learners and adult learners will have equal opportunities to learn and develop within their already existing local school.

groups.title: Which TWO are mentioned by the writer of the passage?
groups.desc:
Questions 11 and 12

Choose TWO correct answers.

The list below includes factors contributing to classroom noise.

collect_option.title: Which TWO are mentioned by the writer of the passage?

questions.analysis: 根据题目要求的 'TWO'，答案 A 和 C 是正确的。从阅读段落中可以看到,A 'Modern teaching practices' 和 C 'mechanical means of ventilation such as air-conditioning units' 都是作者提到的原因,导致了教室中的噪音问题。而其他选项如'echoing corridors'、'large class sizes'和'loud-voiced teachers'并没有在原文中被明确提及。因此,通过分析题目关键词和原文关键信息,可以得出 A 和 C 是符合题目要求的正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Modern teaching practices, the organizations of desks in the classroom, poor classroom acoustics, and mechanical means of ventilation such as air-conditioning units all contribute to the number of children unable to comprehend the teacher's voice.
central_sentences.translation: 现代教学实践、教室中的桌椅布置、糟糕的教室声学条件，以及诸如空调设备等机械通风方式，都导致了更多的儿童无法理解教师的声音。

Only limited attention appears to have been given to those students experiencing the other disabilities involving auditory function deficit.

questions.title: What is the writer's overall purpose in writing this article?

options.text: to compare different methods of dealing with auditory problems
to provide solutions for overly noisy learning environments
to increase awareness of the situation of children with auditory problems
to promote New Zealand as a model for other countries to follow

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C，因为文章的主要目的是提高对有听觉问题的孩子们的关注。文章多次提到听觉问题对孩子们的影响以及需要引起重视，这与C选项“to increase awareness of the situation of children with auditory problems”相对应。

central_sentences.sentence: It is imperative that the needs of these children are taken into account in the setting of appropriate international standards to be promulgated in future.
central_sentences.translation: 在未来制定适当的国际标准时，必须考虑这些儿童的需求。

target_sen.target_sen: It is imperative that the needs of these children are taken into account in the setting of appropriate international standards to be promulgated in future.

content.title: Venus in transit
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了2004年6月8日，全球超过一半的人口目睹了一次罕见的天文事件——金星在太阳表面缓慢移动的“凌日”现象，这是自1882年12月6日以来的首次。当时，美国天文学家西蒙·纽科姆教授率领一支观测团队前往南非观测此事件，他们驻扎在一所女子学校，据称，三位女教师的观测准确度超过了专业人士。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了几个世纪以来，金星凌日吸引了探险家和天文学家前往世界各地，而这一切都要归功于卓越的多才多艺者埃德蒙·哈雷。哈雷在1677年11月在南太平洋的荒岛圣赫勒拍摄到了内行星水星的凌日现象。他意识到，从不同的纬度观测，行星在太阳盘面上的通过会有所不同。通过在两个相距较远的地点计时凌日，天文学家团队可以计算出视差角度——由于观测者位置的差异而导致的天体位置的表观差异。计算出这个角度将使天文学家能够测量当时的终极目标：地球与太阳的距离，即所谓的“天文单位”或AU。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了Halley意识到天文学中最基本的测量之一是天文单位（AU），并且他意识到通过测量金星可以计算出太阳的距离，但金星凌日的发生频率较低，需要等待很长时间。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了受到哈雷建议的启发，英国和法国的天文学家们分别前往印度和西伯利亚等地进行观测，但由于英法两国处于战争状态，事情并没有得到帮助。值得同情的人是法国天文学家吉约姆·勒让蒂尔，他因为英国人围困他在印度的观测点波尔迪沙里而受阻。在一艘法国战舰上逃离印度洋时，勒让蒂尔看到了一次美妙的凌日，但船只的摇晃使得他无法进行准确观测。然而，他并未气馁，继续留在赤道以南，忙于研究毛里求斯和马达加斯加的岛屿，然后前往菲律宾观测下一次凌日。讽刺的是，经过近5万公里的旅行，他在最后一刻被云层遮挡，这是一次非常令人沮丧的经历。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了早期的凌日观测由于“黑点效应”和光的衍射而无法获得准确的时间测量。', 'F': '这段话主要讲了天文学家通过观察金星凌日的远征结果来分析数据，最终确定了一个基于视差测量的天文单位值，这个值相对准确，接近今天的精确度，天文单位是宇宙测量的基准，也是我们今天衡量宇宙的方法的基础，视差原理可以扩展到测量星星的距离。', 'G': '2004年6月的金星凌日是一场天文壮观的景象，而不是一个科学上重要的事件，但这样的凌日为探测其他恒星周围的地球大小行星铺平了道路，这可能是宇宙中最重要的突破之一。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: On 8 June 2004, more than half the population of the world were treated to a rare astronomical event. For over six hours, the planet Venus steadily inched its way over the surface of the Sun. This 'transit' of Venus was the first since 6 December 1882. On that occasion, the American astronomer Professor Simon Newcomb led a party to South Africa to observe the event. They were based at a girls' school, where—it is alleged—the combined forces of three schoolmistresses outperformed the professionals with the accuracy of their observations.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: For centuries, transits of Venus have drawn explorers and astronomers alike to the four corners of the globe. And you can put it all down to the extraordinary polymath Edmond Halley. In November 1677, Halley observed a transit of the innermost planet, Mercury, from the desolate island of St Helena in the South Pacific. He realized that, from different latitudes, the passage of the planet across the Sun's disc would appear to differ. By timing the transit from two widely-separated locations, teams of astronomers could calculate the parallax angle—the apparent difference in position of an astronomical body due to a difference in the observer's position. Calculating this angle would allow astronomers to measure what was then the ultimate goal: the distance of the Earth from the Sun. This distance is known as the 'astronomical unit' or AU.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Halley was aware that the AU was one of the most fundamental of all astronomical measurements. Johannes Kepler, in the early 17th century, had shown that the distances of the planets from the Sun governed their orbital speeds, which were easily measurable. But no-one had found a way to calculate accurate distances to the planets from the Earth. The goal was to measure the AU; then, knowing the orbital speeds of all the other planets round the Sun, the scale of the Solar System would fall into place. However, Halley realised that Mercury was so far away that its parallax angle would be very difficult to determine. As Venus was closer to the Earth, its parallax angle would be larger, and Halley worked out that by using Venus it would be possible to measure the Sun's distance to 1 part in 500. But there was a problem: transits of Venus, unlike those of Mercury, are rare, occurring in pairs roughly eight years apart every hundred or so years. Nevertheless, he accurately predicted that Venus would cross the face of the Sun in both 1761 and 1769—though he didn't survive to see either.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Inspired by Halley's suggestion of a way to pin down the scale of the Solar System, teams of British and French astronomers set out on expeditions to places as diverse as India and Siberia. But things weren't helped by Britain and France being at war. The person who deserves most sympathy is the French astronomer Guillaume Le Gentil. He was thwarted by the fact that the British were besieging his observation site at Pondicherry in India. Fleeing on a French warship crossing the Indian Ocean, Le Gentil saw a wonderful transit—but the ship's pitching and rolling ruled out any attempt at making accurate observations. Undaunted, he remained south of the equator, keeping himself busy by studying the islands of Mauritius and Madagascar before setting off to observe the next transit in the Philippines. Ironically after travelling nearly 50,000 kilometers, his view was clouded out at the last moment, a very dispiriting experience.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: While the early transit timings were as precise as instruments would allow, the measurements were dogged by the 'black drop' effect. When Venus begins to cross the Sun's disc, it looks smeared not circular—which makes it difficult to establish timings. This is due to diffraction of light. The second problem is that Venus exhibits a halo of light when it is seen just outside the Sun's disc. While this showed astronomers that Venus was surrounded by a thick layer of gases refracting sunlight around it, both effects made it impossible to obtain accurate timings.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: But astronomers laboured hard to analyse the results of these expeditions to observe Venus transits. Johann Franz Encke, Director of the Berlin Observatory, finally determined a value for the AU based on all these parallax measurements: 153,340,000 km. Reasonably accurate for the time, that is quite close to today's value of methods in accuracy. The AU is a cosmic measuring rod, and the basis of how we scale the Universe today. The parallax principle can be extended to measure the distances to the stars. If we look at a star in January—when Earth is at one point in its orbit—it will seem to be in a different position from where it appears six months later. Knowing the width of Earth's orbit, the parallax shift lets astronomers calculate the distance.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: June 2004's transit of Venus was thus more of an astronomical spectacle than a scientifically important event. But such transits have paved the way for what might prove to be one of the most vital breakthroughs in the cosmos—detecting Earth-sized planets orbiting other stars.

content.title: A neuroscientist reveals how to think differently

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: In the last decade a revolution has occurred in the way that scientists think about the brain. We now know that the decisions humans make can be traced to the firing patterns of neurons in specific parts of the brain. These discoveries have led to the field known as neuroeconomics, which studies the brain's secrets to success in an economic environment that demands innovation and being able to do things differently from competitors. A brain that can do this is an iconoclastic one. Briefly, an iconoclast is a person who does something that others say can't be done.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: This definition implies that iconoclasts are different from other people, but more precisely, it is the brains that are different in three distinct ways: perception, fear response, and social intelligence. Each of these three functions utilizes a different circuit in the brain. Naysayers might suggest that the brain is irrelevant, that thinking in an original, even revolutionary, way is more a matter of personality than brain function. But the field of neuroeconomics was born out of the realization that the physical workings of the brain place limitations on the way we make decisions. By understanding these constraints, we begin to understand why some people march to a different drumbeat.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The first thing to realize is that the brain suffers from limited resources. It has a fixed energy budget, about the same as a 40 watt light bulb, so it has evolved to work as efficiently as possible. This is where most people are impeded from being an iconoclast. For example, when confronted with information streaming from the eyes, the brain will interpret this information in the quickest way possible. Thus it will draw on both past experience and any other source of information, such as what other people say, to make sense of what it is seeing. This happens all the time. The brain takes shortcuts that work so well we are hardly ever aware of them. We think our perceptions of the world are real, but they are only biological and electrical rumblings. Perception is not simply a product of what your eyes or ears transmit to your brain. More than the physical reality of photons or sound waves, perception is a product of the brain.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Perception is central to iconoclasm. Iconoclasts see things differently from other people. Their brains do not fall into efficiency pitfalls as much as the average person's brain. Iconoclasts, either because they were born that way or through learning, have found ways to work around the perceptual shortcuts that plague most people. Perception is not something that is hardwired into the brain. It is a learned process, which is both a curse and an opportunity for change. The brain faces the fundamental problem of interpreting physical stimuli from the senses. Everything the brain sees, hears, or touches has multiple interpretations. The one that is ultimately chosen is simply the brain's best theory. In technical terms, these conjectures have their basis in the statistical likelihood of one interpretation over another and are heavily influenced by past experience and, importantly for potential iconoclasts, what other people say.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The best way to see things differently from other people is to bombard the brain with things it has never encountered before. Novelty releases the perceptual process from the chains of past experience and forces the brain to make new judgments. Successful iconoclasts have an extraordinary willingness to be exposed to what is fresh and different. Observation of iconoclasts shows that they embrace novelty while most people avoid things that are different.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The problem with novelty, however, is that it tends to trigger the brain's fear system. Fear is a major impediment to thinking like an iconoclast and stops the average person in his tracks. There are many types of fear, but the two that inhibit iconoclastic thinking and people generally find difficult to deal with are fear of uncertainty and fear of public ridicule. These may seem like trivial phobias. But fear of public speaking, which everyone must do from time to time, afflicts one-third of the population. This makes it too common to be considered a mental disorder. It is simply a common variant of human nature, one which iconoclasts do not let inhibit their reactions.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Finally, to be successful iconoclasts, individuals must sell their ideas to other people. This is where social intelligence comes in. Social intelligence is the ability to understand and manage people in a business setting. In the last decade there has been an explosion of knowledge about the social brain and how the brain works when groups coordinate decision making. Neuroscience has revealed which brain circuits are responsible for functions like understanding what other people think, empathy, fairness, and social identity. These brain regions play key roles in whether people convince others of their ideas. Perception is important in social cognition too. The perception of someone's enthusiasm, or reputation, can make or break a deal. Understanding how perception becomes intertwined with social decision making shows why successful iconoclasts are so rare.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Iconoclasts create new opportunities in every area from artistic expression to technology to business. They supply creativity and innovation not easily accomplished by committees. Rules aren't important to them. Iconoclasts face alienation and failure, but can also be a major asset to any organization. It is crucial for success in any field to understand how the iconoclastic mind works.

questions.title: Neuroeconomics is a field of study which seeks to

options.text: cause a change in how scientists understand brain chemistry.
understand how good decisions are made in the brain.
understand how the brain is linked to achievement in competitive fields.
trace the specific firing patterns of neurons in different areas of the brain.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为文章明确指出neuroeconomics研究的是在竞争环境中取得成功的脑部秘密。通过找到题干和文章中的同义表达，使用关键词法和排除法，学生可以更容易地确定正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: These discoveries have led to the field known as neuroeconomics, which studies the brain's secrets to success in an economic environment that demands innovation and being able to do things differently from competitors.
central_sentences.translation: 这些发现导致了被称为神经经济学的领域，该领域研究大脑在需要创新和能够与竞争对手不同地行事的经济环境中取得成功的秘密。

target_sen.target_sen: These discoveries have led to the field known as neuroeconomics, which studies the brain's secrets to success in an economic environment that demands innovation and being able to do things differently from competitors.

These discoveries have led to the field known as neuroeconomics, which studies the brain's secrets to success in an economic environment that demands innovation and being able to do things differently from competitors.

questions.title: According to the writer, iconoclasts are distinctive because
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B: their brains function differently。文章明确指出反传统者与众不同的原因在于他们的大脑功能不同，而不是个性或其他因素。通过关键词对比和排除法，即使词汇量有限的学生也能做对这道题。

central_sentences.sentence: more precisely, it is the brains that are different in three distinct ways: perception, fear response, and social intelligence. Each
central_sentences.translation: 更准确地说，是他们的大脑在三个不同方面有所不同：感知、恐惧反应和社交智能。

target_sen.target_sen: This definition implies that iconoclasts are different from other people, but more precisely, it is the brains that are different in three distinct ways: perception, fear response, and social intelligence.
Each of these three functions utilizes a different circuit in the brain.
By understanding these constraints, we begin to understand why some people march to a different drumbeat.

This definition implies that iconoclasts are different from other people, but more precisely, it is the brains that are different in three distinct ways: perception, fear response, and social intelligence.

questions.title: According to the writer, the brain works efficiently because
questions.analysis: 文章中提到大脑由于资源有限，需要高效工作，并通过依赖过去的经验来快速解释接收到的信息。这与题干中的“brain works efficiently”直接对应。因此，正确答案是 D: it relies on previous events。

central_sentences.sentence: Thus it will draw on both past experience and any other source of information, such as what other people say, to make sense of what it is seeing.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，为了理解所看到的东西，大脑会依赖过去的经验和其他信息来源，比如别人说的话。

target_sen.target_sen: It has a fixed energy budget, about the same as a 40 watt light bulb, so it has evolved to work as efficiently as possible.
For example, when confronted with information streaming from the eyes, the brain will interpret this information in the quickest way possible.

questions.title: The writer says that perception is

options.text: combination of photons and sound waves
a reliable product of what your senses transmit.
a process we are usually conscious of.

questions.analysis: 文章明确指出，感知（perception）不仅仅是感官传递的信息，而是大脑处理的结果。这与选项C "a result of brain processes" 完全对应。因此，正确答案是C。

central_sentences.sentence: More than the physical reality of photons or sound waves, perception is a product of the brain.
central_sentences.translation: 感知不仅仅是光子或声波的物理现实，更是大脑的产物。

target_sen.target_sen: Perception is not simply a product of what your eyes or ears transmit to your brain.

questions.title: According to the writer, an iconoclastic thinker

options.text: centralizes perceptual thinking in one part of the brain.
has a brain that is hardwired for learning.
has more opportunities than the average person.

questions.analysis: 文章中明确提到，反传统者（iconoclasts）看待事物的方式与其他人不同，他们的大脑不像普通人的大脑那样容易陷入效率陷阱（efficiency pitfalls）。这与选项B“avoids cognitive traps”是同义表达。因此，正确答案是B。

central_sentences.sentence: Iconoclasts see things differently to other people. Their brains do not fail into efficiency pitfalls as much as the average person's brain. Iconoclasts
central_sentences.translation: 标新立异者看待事物的方式与其他人不同。与普通人相比，他们的大脑不太容易陷入效率陷阱。

target_sen.target_sen: In technical terms, these conjectures have their basis in the statistical likelihood of one interpretation over another and are heavily influenced by past experience and, importantly for potential iconoclasts, what other people say.

groups.desc:
Questions 32-37

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer,
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer,
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

questions.title: Exposure to different events forces the brain to think differently.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Exposure to different events forces the brain to think differently" 与原文中的 "bombard the brain with things it has never encountered before" 和 "forces the brain to make new judgments" 直接对应。题干中的 "Exposure" 对应 "bombard"，"different events" 对应 "things it has never encountered before"，"forces" 对应 "forces"，"think differently" 对应 "make new judgments"。因此，题干中的陈述在原文中得到了明确的支持，答案是 YES / TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: The best way to see things differently to other people is to bombard the brain with things it has never encouraged before. Novelty releases the perceptual process from the chains of past experience and forces the brain to make new judgments. Successful
central_sentences.translation: 让人以不同的方式看待事物的最佳方法是用大脑从未遇到过的事物轰炸大脑。新奇的事物将感知过程从过去经验的束缚中释放出来，并迫使大脑做出新的判断。

syn_ary.synonym: things it has never encountered before

target_sen.target_sen: The best way to see things differently to other people is to bombard the brain with things it has never encouraged before.
Novelty releases the perceptual process from the chains of past experience and forces the brain to make new judgments.
Novelty releases the perceptual process from the chains of past experience and forces the brain to make new judgments.

questions.title: Iconoclasts are usually receptive to new experiences.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "receptive to new experiences" 在原文中通过 "embrace novelty" 和 "willingness to be exposed to what is fresh and different" 得到了对应和解释。原文明确指出反传统者（iconoclasts）接受新奇事物（novelty），这与题干中的 "receptive to new experiences" 是同义表达。因此，正确答案为 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: Observation of iconoclasts shows that they embrace novelty while most people avoid things that are different.
central_sentences.translation: 对反传统者的观察表明，他们接受新事物，而大多数人则避免不同的事物。

target_sen.target_sen: Successful iconoclasts have an extraordinary willingness to be exposed to what is fresh and different.
Observation of iconoclasts shows that they embrace novelty while most people avoid things that are different.
Observation of iconoclasts shows that they embrace novelty while most people avoid things that are different.

syn_ary.synonym: extraordinary willingness to be exposed to what is fresh and different

target_sen.target_sen: Successful iconoclasts have an extraordinary willingness to be exposed to what is fresh and different.

questions.title: Most people are too shy to try different things.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Most people are too shy to try different things”意思是大多数人因为害羞而不尝试新事物。文章中提到“Fear is a major impediment to thinking like an iconoclast and stops the average person in his tracks”，这里提到的是“fear”而不是“shy”，并且没有明确说大多数人因为害羞而不尝试新事物。因此，题干中的信息在文章中没有明确提到，正确答案是“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.sentence: Fear is a major impediment to thinking like an iconoclast and stops the average person in his tracks.
central_sentences.translation: 恐惧是阻碍像特立独行者那样思考的主要障碍，它会让普通人停滞不前。

target_sen.target_sen: Fear is a major impediment to thinking like an iconoclast and stops the average person in his tracks.
Fear is a major impediment to thinking like an iconoclast and stops the average person in his tracks.

questions.title: If you think in an iconoclastic way, you can easily overcome fear.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“iconoclastic way”在原文中对应“thinking like an iconoclast”，而“easily overcome fear”在原文中对应“difficult to deal with”和“fear is a major impediment”。原文明确指出恐惧是反传统思维的主要障碍，并且人们普遍难以应对恐惧，这与题干中“轻松克服恐惧”的说法相反。因此，正确答案是“NO”。

central_sentences.sentence: Fear is a major impediment to thinking like an iconoclast and stops the average person in his tracks. There are many types of fear, but the two that inhibit iconoclastic thinking and people generally find difficult to deal with are fear of uncertainty and fear of public ridicule.
central_sentences.translation: 恐惧是反传统思维的一个主要障碍，它会让普通人止步不前。有许多类型的恐惧，但其中两种会抑制反传统思维，并且人们通常发现很难应对，它们是对不确定性的恐惧和对公众嘲笑的恐惧。

target_sen.target_sen: Fear is a major impediment to thinking like an iconoclast and stops the average person in his tracks.

syn_ary.synonym: Fear is a major impediment to thinking like an iconoclast

target_sen.target_sen: Fear is a major impediment to thinking like an iconoclast and stops the average person in his tracks.

syn_ary.synonym: people generally find difficult to deal with

target_sen.target_sen: There are many types of fear, but the two that inhibit iconoclastic thinking and people generally find difficult to deal with are fear of uncertainty and fear of public ridicule.

questions.title: When concern about embarrassment matters less, other fears become irrelevant.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息在文章中没有明确提到，也没有相反的信息。文章提到恐惧是阻碍非传统思维的主要因素，但没有具体说明当对尴尬的担忧减少时，其他恐惧是否变得无关紧要。因此，正确答案是 NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: There are many types of fear, but the two that inhibit iconoclastic thinking and people generally find difficult to deal with are fear of uncertainty and fear of public ridicule.
central_sentences.translation: 有许多类型的恐惧，但抑制非传统思维且人们通常难以应对的两种是对不确定性的恐惧和对公众嘲笑的恐惧。

target_sen.target_sen: There are many types of fear, but the two that inhibit iconoclastic thinking and people generally find difficult to deal with are fear of uncertainty and fear of public ridicule.
The problem with novelty, however, is that it tends to trigger the brain's fear system.
There are many types of fear, but the two that inhibit iconoclastic thinking and people generally find difficult to deal with are fear of uncertainty and fear of public ridicule.
But fear of public speaking, which everyone must do from time to time, afflicts one-third of the population.

questions.title: Fear of public speaking is a psychological illness.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Fear of public speaking is a psychological illness" 表示公众演讲恐惧是一种心理疾病。而原文中明确指出 "fear of public speaking" 太常见了，不能被认为是一种 "mental disorder"（心理障碍）。因此，题干中的说法是错误的，正确答案是 NO。

central_sentences.sentence: This makes it too common to be considered a mental disorder.
central_sentences.translation: 这使得它太普遍了，不能被认为是一种心理疾病。

target_sen.target_sen: But fear of public speaking, which everyone must do from time to time, afflicts one-third of the population.
This makes it too common to be considered a mental disorder.

groups.desc:
Questions 38-40

Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A-E, below.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: requires both perceptual and social intelligence skills.
focuses on how groups decide on an action.
works in many fields, both artistic and scientific.
leaves one open to criticism and rejection.
involves understanding how organizations manage people.

questions.title: Thinking like a successful iconoclast is demanding because it
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Thinking like a successful iconoclast is demanding because it”可以通过文章中的“successful iconoclasts”来定位。文章明确指出，成功的反传统者在感知、恐惧反应和社交智力方面与众不同，这与选项A中的“requires both perceptual and social intelligence skills”直接对应。因此，正确答案是A。

central_sentences.sentence: This definition implies that iconoclasts are different from other people, but more precisely, it is the brains that are different in three distinct ways: perception, fear response, and social intelligence.
central_sentences.translation: 这个定义暗示了反传统者与其他人不同，但更准确地说，是他们的大脑在三个方面有所不同：感知、恐惧反应和社交智能。

target_sen.target_sen: This definition implies that iconoclasts are different from other people, but more precisely, it is the brains that are different in three distinct ways: perception, fear response, and social intelligence.
Each of these three functions utilizes a different circuit in the brain.
By understanding these constraints, we begin to understand why some people march to a different drumbeat.

questions.title: The concept of the social brain is useful to iconoclasts because it
questions.analysis: 正确答案是 B，因为文章中明确提到 "the social brain" 的作用是帮助理解 "how the brain works when groups coordinate decision making"，这与选项 B 中的 "focuses on how groups decide on an action" 是同义表达。通过关键词定位和同义替换法，即使词汇量较差的学生也能做对这道题。

central_sentences.sentence: In the last decade there has been an explosion of knowledge about the social brain and how the brain works when groups coordinate decision making.
central_sentences.translation: 在过去十年中，关于社会大脑以及大脑在群体协调决策时如何工作的知识出现了爆炸性增长。

target_sen.target_sen: In the last decade there has been an explosion of knowledge about the social brain and how the brain works when groups coordinate decision making.
Finally, to be successful iconoclasts, individuals must sell their ideas to other people.

questions.title: Iconoclasts are generally an asset because their way of thinking
questions.analysis: 题干问的是为什么Iconoclasts（打破传统的人）是资产。文章中提到他们在多个领域（包括艺术和科学）创造了新的机会，这与题干中的“their way of thinking works in many fields, both artistic and scientific”相对应。因此，正确答案是C。

central_sentences.sentence: Iconoclasts create new opportunities in every area from artistic expression to technology to business. They supply creativity and innovation not easily accomplished by committees.
central_sentences.translation: 打破传统的人在从艺术表达到技术再到商业的各个领域创造新机会。他们提供了委员会难以实现的创造力和创新。

target_sen.target_sen: Iconoclasts face alienation and failure, but can also be a major asset to any organization.
Iconoclasts create new opportunities in every area from artistic expression to technology to business.
They supply creativity and innovation not easily accomplished by committees.
